name: Pecan Caramel Clusters
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the light cream in a heavy 2-quart saucepan to lukewarm (110°F). Reserve half of the cream.
2. Add sugar, corn syrup, and salt to the remaining cream in the saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils.
3. Slowly stir in the reserved cream. Cook, stirring constantly, for 5 minutes.
4. Stir in butter one teaspoon at a time, continuing to stir constantly. Heat until the mixture reaches the soft ball stage (234°F) on a candy thermometer.
5. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla.
6. Arrange pecans on a waxed-paper lined baking sheet. Drop spoonfuls of the mixture onto the pecans.
7. As clusters cool, use a metal spatula to transfer them to another waxed-paper lined baking sheet.
8. Push remaining pecans together and repeat until all candy mixture is used. If thick, reheat gently.
9. Melt chocolate chips over hot water and stir until smooth. Spread over each cluster.
10. Let stand until set before storing in a cool place. Makes about 4 dozen.
